Software Engineering: Builds software applications and systems using programming languages like Python, Java, C++, C#, and JavaScript. Works with web frameworks (React, FastAPI, Flask, Django, Express.js), databases (MongoDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL), cloud and DevOps (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, AWS), APIs and microservices. Develops full-stack applications, integrates AI/ML models into software products, and applies software engineering best practices including OOP, DSA, system design, and version control.
Data Science: Focuses on statistical analysis, predictive modeling, and building machine learning pipelines. Works primarily with data processing libraries (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn), trains and evaluates ML/DL models (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras), builds data visualization dashboards (Tableau, Matplotlib), and performs experimental analysis on structured datasets. Primary role is analyzing data, not building software products.
Finance: Works in banking, investment, accounting, financial analysis, portfolio management, risk assessment, auditing, and financial modeling using Excel and Bloomberg.
Healthcare: Works in medicine, nursing, clinical research, pharmacy, medical devices, patient care, health informatics, or public health.
Marketing: Manages brand strategy, digital marketing, SEO, social media, content creation, campaign analytics, CRM, and growth marketing.
Law: Practices legal work including litigation, contracts, compliance, intellectual property, corporate law, and legal research.
Education: Works in teaching, curriculum development, academic research, instructional design, e-learning, or educational administration.
Mechanical Engineering: Designs and manufactures mechanical systems, works with CAD tools, thermodynamics, materials science, robotics, and manufacturing processes.
Cybersecurity: Handles network security, penetration testing, threat analysis, SIEM tools, vulnerability assessment, and security compliance.
Product Management: Defines product roadmaps, works with cross-functional teams, conducts user research, manages sprints, and drives product strategy.
